text:
There once was a quirky little inventor named Harold. Every afternoon after tinkering in his workshop for hours, Harold would retreat to a small, cozy nook in his living room filled with stuffed animals to hear his favorite music. One day, Harold invented something extraordinary—what he called a "Time Whisperer."

The Time Whisperer was an old, velvet-covered doll that Harold suspected had never moved since he got it as a child. It had two silver eyes that blinked when you talked to it. Harold decided it could help him achieve his wildest inventions. He'd talk to it about dreams.

On Harold's birthday, he surprised himself by singing about his dream of flying. The Time Whisperer just blinked. The next day, Harold dreamed about becoming the first human to skateboard on water. He talked to the doll again. It blinked affirmatively.

Over time, Harold became friends with his little critter. Together, they envisioned a magical bridge across oceans, powered by the energy of dreams.
